=Level 472= {{Class 0|class= 4|hexagon= 4|header= Threat Index: Class|safety= Unsafe|security= Unstable|entity= Mentally Hazardous Entities
» Environmental Hazards|color-h=46.67|color-s=50%|color-l=14.12%}}
==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Deep in the Jungle.jpg|caption=An image of the level. Wanderer U}} '''Level 472''' is a massive jungle, appearing to be of Southeast Asian origin. The level is permanently shrouded in darkness, with no day or night cycle. The level, although with an abundance of flora, has no fauna, save for one entity species unique to this level that we'll delve down into later. The level has very dense foliage that makes traversal very difficult at times, and it is recommended you bring a machete or similar tools to this level (however it is advised to avoid this level either way). '''Level 472''' also has a low cloud ceiling which causes periodic fog and mist. The level is said to give a feeling of paranoia, regardless of whether you've encountered the entity. '''Level 472''' has 2 other structures other than the jungle, which we will talk about in a bit. The level has many environmental hazards, most notably traps. The forest floor is littered with them, and you always have to make a careful step. The traps are lethal, and if you don't die to them it sure as hell will hurt a lot. To this day no one knows who sets up these traps, whether it's and entity, a wanderer, or the level itself.
'''>FILE-0472-A:traps_472.zip=compressed'''
'''>FILE-0472-A:traps_472.zip=uncompressed'''
{{Underline|191a1a}}
'''Punji Sticks'''
{{Blurimage|file=Punjisticks.jpeg|caption=A photo of a punji stick trap. Wanderer PD|align=center}}Punji Sticks are sharp sticks dug in a small pit, covered by a thin layer of leaves and mud. They are designed so that when the victim steps on them, their foot goes through the covering and into the sticks. Normally this trap wouldn't be lethal, however the sticks are coated and soaked in '''[[Object_12|Liquid Pain]]''', making them very deadly.

'''Tripwire'''
{{Blurimage|file=Tripwiregrenade.jpg|caption=An old photo of a tripwire grenade trap, dated 1971. Wanderer U|align=center}}Tripwire traps are simple: a wire is strung across a trail or other commonly used walkway. The wire, when disturbed, activates another trap, be it a pitfall, an arrow, or even a grenade or mine! Although rudimentary, tripwires are easy to work with, and have a million ways to be deadly.

'''Bamboo Whip'''
{{Blurimage|file=Bamboowhip.jpeg|caption=A recreation of a bamboo whip trap in [[Level 122]]. Wanderer CA|align=center}}Bamboo Whips are bamboo poles that have sharp spikes at the top that are also coated in '''[[Object_12|Liquid Pain]]'''. These poles would be stuck in areas such as the ground or in trees and pulled back until the tension is as high as possible without breaking the bamboo. When the trap is triggered, via tripwire or other means, the bamboo pole would snap back at speeds of over 100 mph, impaling the victim and possibly even cracking or breaking bones.

This is just a small amount of traps that must be looked out for, and many more exist.
{{Underline|191a1a}}
{{Blurimage|file=Deep jungle.jpg|caption=Another, clearer image of the level. Wanderer PD|align=left}}Due to the levels thick foliage and unstable nature, mapping '''Level 472''''s terrain has proved to be virtually impossible, making navigation very difficult. Also due to the levels unstable nature, it is easy to get lost and even start walking circles. Exit for this level is quite difficult, and traversing the wide expanse is nothing short of hell. However, there are things in the level outside of the massive junglescape- '''FOBs'''Forward Operating Bases- Temporary military camps made in the opponent's territory and '''POW'''Prisoner Of War- Soldiers who surrendered and were taken prisoner by the enemy '''Camps'''. ===FOBs=== FOBs are small, leveled areas, about 100 yds^2 in area. They contain tents, material/resource depots, helipads (some with helicopters in them), artillery, etc. They also have many items such as American weaponry and ordinance (e.g. M16A1s, M60s, M26s), gasoline and diesel, MREs, and even napalm, as well as materials such as wood and steel. ===POW Camps=== POW Camps are similar to FOBs, however they are much more dangerous. POW Camps are much more permanent, containing garages, hangars, guard and prisoner barracks, lookout towers, helipads, material/resource depots, quarries, etc. They also have similar items to the FOBs such as Vietnamese weapons and ordinance (e.g. AK-47s, RPKs, F1s), gasoline and diesel, Vietnamese aircraft, food, and materials. While similar to FOBs, POW Camps are much more dangerous due to the entities. ==Entities== '''Level 472''' has one unique species of entity:
Entity

'''Speakers'''

'''Habitat: Throughout Level 472, most commonly POW Camps
Aggression: 4/5 - Not documented with other entities, however is persistent with attacks
Intelligence: ?/5 - Not Documented
Danger: 4/5 - Life-threatening, but can be evaded without alerting'''

'''Total Threat Level: 4'''
==Entity Description== '''Speakers''' are massive entities that roam around the jungle of '''Level 472'''. Their towering bodies seem to be made of a metal-like substance, and their form represents a radio tower with horns at the top. So far, no '''speakers''' have been killed. '''Speakers''' are quite mysterious entities, as not many people have been able to witness or document them without being killed. ==Behavior== '''Speakers''' tend to gravitate towards POW Camps, and sometimes FOBs. Once they have claimed an area, it is seen that they do not move until they come across their prey. Also, it might be worth noting that other '''speakers''' seem to avoid the general vicinity of claimed areas. When coming across their prey, they will remain stationary and begin to play radio broadcasts, not unlike you'd see on your average FM signal. These broadcasts have been identified as the famous Vietnam War broadcasts from [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hanoi_Hannah Hanoi Hanna], an iconic figure at the time. These will repeat continuously, starting normal and increasing in volume as time goes on. These broadcasts cause rapid mental deterioration, along with being able to plant feelings of trauma, hopelessness, depression, and other things, which increase in severity over time, until the victim is dead. If the victim tries to escape, the '''speaker''' will arise and give chase. {| class="article-table" style="border: 2px solid #362e12; margin:0;" |+ |1 minute of exposure |style="border-left:solid 2px #362e12" | Victim will start experiencing mild discomfort, with paranoia increasing. | |- |2 minutes of exposure |style="border-left:solid 2px #362e12" | Victim will start feeling depressed, and panic will start. | |- |3 minutes of exposure |style="border-left:solid 2px #362e12" | Victim will begin to have minor headaches, trauma sets in, along with panic
and paranoia increasing in severity. | |- |4 minutes of exposure |style="border-left:solid 2px #362e12" | Victim will experience severe headaches and start to hallucinate, the panic
will be overwhelming. | |- |5+ minutes of exposure |style="border-left:solid 2px #362e12" | Victim will fall unconscious due to the overwhelming headaches, hallucinations,
and panic. At this point, the victim is considered dead. | |} It is not known what happens after a '''speaker''' kills it's victim, or why it kills them at all. After it is done, it will proceed to leave the area it had claimed, leaving it safe to explore until another '''speaker''' claims it. ==Biology== The biology of the '''speakers''' in unknown
==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== * Noclipping in [[Level 6.1]] will bring you to this level (however, noclipping in Level 6.1 is difficult) * Wandering throughout the Jungle of [[Level 110]] has a chance to gradually become '''Level 472''', via the time fading to night ===Exits=== * Noclipping through the ground in the FOBs or POW Camps, although rare, will send you to either [[Level 11]] or [[Level 245]] * Noclipping in the jungle will lead you back to Level 110 * Getting lost in the fog might transport you to [[Level 155]] {{Footnotes}}
